mechanism_narrative: A falling three-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is measured continuously.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.08 as of one month before decision date
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if lower three-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher changes in at least 60% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 7.0 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS rise together over three months, stronger consumer spending is backed by job creation, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both exceed their values from 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:PAYEMS
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 432310 as of one month before decision date
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 136765 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-PAYEMS trigger fails to outperform cases where either series is not rising over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO paired with rising CPIAUCSL indicates margin pressure from weaker output and higher input costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is below its value from 3 months earlier while CPIAUCSL is above its value from 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 99.9778 as of two months before decision date
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 233.808 as of two months before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have lower 60-day forward S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the sign of the return spread is positive in most walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in HOUST signals contraction in interest-sensitive construction activity and weaker future durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 883.0 as of four months before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-crossing episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or by returns above the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in GDPC1 combined with rising RSAFS identifies broad real demand expansion, increasing expected earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its prior quarterly value and RSAFS is above its value from 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:RSAFS
fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 15819.0 as of five months before decision date
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 432310 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS expansion trigger has a non-positive average 60-day forward return or underperforms the unconditional 60-day return in most rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Very low FEDFUNDS combined with rising PAYEMS indicates accommodative policy while labor income is improving, increasing equity risk tolerance and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and PAYEMS is above its value from 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:PAYEMS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.08 as of one month before decision date
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 136765 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-rate labor-improvement observations have lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than low-rate observations without PAYEMS improvement across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in UNRATE signals improving labor-market momentum and lower near-term recession risk, supporting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 7.0 as of one month before decision date
failure_condition: Reject if downward UNRATE crossings are followed by non-positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of triggered observations or show Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.
